claim_status: No-claim sandbox probe inventory only; no sandbox-readiness claim, no renderer-security claim, no site-isolation claim, no hostile-browsing safety claim, no platform containment claim, no packaged-build probe claim, no effective-policy claim, no SEC-GATE-1 claim, no SEC-GATE-6 claim, and no production-safety claim.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt arbitrary filesystem read, arbitrary filesystem write, path traversal, symlink or junction escape, and cross-profile file access.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny or return a bounded policy error unless a broker-issued, task-scoped handle authorizes the exact operation.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No packaged role process, broker fixture, path-race test, effective policy capture, or probe result log exists.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt ambient TCP, UDP, DNS, proxy bypass, loopback listener, and destination not covered by a kernel-issued request context.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny ambient socket access; the network role may use sockets only through authorized request context checks.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No per-role network namespace, AppContainer, seatbelt, seccomp, broker, or socket-denial probe exists.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt child process creation, shell execution, job escape, fork/exec, service launch, and inherited-handle abuse.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny process creation and inherited authority unless the browser kernel deliberately launches an allowed role.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No role launcher, handle allowlist inspection, process-creation denial probe, or packaged result artifact exists.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt Windows registry or platform configuration reads and writes outside explicitly brokered policy.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny registry/configuration access or return unsupported on non-Windows platforms without treating the probe as passed.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No Windows effective-token, AppContainer, registry virtualization, or denial log evidence exists.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt camera, microphone, display capture, USB, Bluetooth, serial, HID, graphics-device, raw input, or other raw device access.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny raw device access unless a broker-issued, user-approved, task-scoped device handle exists.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No packaged platform-device probe, permission fixture, or brokered-device denial evidence exists.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt unbounded shared-memory allocation, executable mapping, writable trusted metadata, stale generation reuse, or cross-process mapping not issued by a trusted service.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny untrusted shared memory and executable mappings; allow only bounded, typed, purpose-specific, revocable regions.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No shared-memory creation broker, generation check, mapping inspection, or stale-handle denial probe exists.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt direct keychain, password vault, cookie store, passkey, client certificate, payment identity, or provider-token access.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny direct credential access; require origin/profile/user-policy mediated handles with redacted diagnostics.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No credential broker, fake vault fixture, platform keychain denial probe, or redaction artifact exists.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt debugger attach, ptrace, process memory read, remote debug listener creation, debug-port binding, crash dump expansion, or release-symbol access.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny debug authority unless a visible, authenticated DevTools or crash path has explicitly scoped permission.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No debugger denial harness, remote-listener probe, visible-indicator proof, or symbol-access denial artifact exists.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt profile directory read/write, cross-profile object access, private-session data access, history/bookmark/cookie database access, or origin-store escape.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny ambient profile access; storage operations must use brokered profile/origin/partition-scoped handles.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No real-profile-free fixture, cross-profile denial harness, storage broker probe, or profile cleanup artifact exists.

required_probe_surfaces.hostile_attempt: Attempt unauthorized service connection, wrong-role message, forged identity, stale epoch, duplicate capability, platform IPC, COM/RPC/Mach/dbus escape, or internal protocol access.
required_probe_surfaces.expected_safe_result: Deny unauthorized IPC and return bounded policy errors without granting authority or hiding connection failures.
required_probe_surfaces.missing_evidence: No canonical IPC schema, connection-authentication proof, platform IPC probe, or compromised-client harness exists.

probe_targets.purpose: Host hostile web content with assigned IPC, assigned shared memory, assigned surfaces, and read-only font handles only.
probe_targets.current_evidence: process-capabilities.json forbids ambient network, arbitrary filesystem, credential store, process creation, debug attach, raw devices, global clipboard, update service, and browser-internal protocol access.
probe_targets.missing_evidence: No real renderer-role packaged process, platform policy, effective-policy capture, or compromised-renderer probe result exists.

probe_matrix.expected_result: Deny arbitrary filesystem and cross-profile paths before broker work.
Deny ambient network sockets and unauthorized loopback listeners.
Deny child process creation, shell execution, and inherited handle abuse.
Deny platform configuration and Windows registry access.
Deny raw devices, camera, microphone, screen capture, and raw input.
Deny unassigned, executable, stale, or oversized shared memory.
Deny keychain, password vault, cookie, passkey, and token access.
Deny debugger attach, memory read, and remote debug listener creation.
Deny profile files and cross-profile storage without brokered logical handles.
Deny unauthorized services, forged role, wrong principal, stale epoch, and internal protocol access.

probe_targets.purpose: Own protocol connections while remaining unable to read password vaults, arbitrary profile files, browser UI, or agent confirmation authority.
probe_targets.current_evidence: process-capabilities.json allows network sockets, proxy resolution, certificate verification adapter, HTTP cache, and cookie attachment while forbidding password vault and arbitrary profile files.
probe_targets.missing_evidence: No real network-role packaged process, request-context verifier, effective platform policy, or ambient-socket bypass probe exists.

probe_matrix.expected_result: Deny arbitrary filesystem and arbitrary profile files outside approved cache roots.
Deny ambient socket use outside kernel-issued request contexts and configured proxy policy.
Deny child process creation and shell execution from network input.
Deny ambient platform configuration edits and registry writes.
Deny raw devices and capture APIs.
Deny unassigned shared memory and executable mappings.
Deny password vault and direct credential secret export.
Deny debugger attach, process memory read, and debug listener creation.
Deny browser history, bookmarks, and arbitrary profile databases.
Deny wrong-role messages, forged request contexts, and unauthorized internal services.

probe_targets.purpose: Own profile storage roots, quota database, and logical origin stores without ambient network or browser UI authority.
probe_targets.current_evidence: process-capabilities.json allows profile storage roots, quota database, and logical origin stores while forbidding network sockets, credential secret export, browser UI, and process creation.
probe_targets.missing_evidence: No storage-role process, origin/profile fixture, migration-safe denial probe, or effective storage sandbox artifact exists.

probe_matrix.expected_result: Deny files outside assigned profile storage roots and origin stores.
Deny network sockets and DNS access from storage input.
Deny child process creation and shell execution.
Deny registry and platform configuration access not explicitly brokered.
Deny raw devices and capture APIs.
Deny unassigned shared memory and executable mappings.
Deny credential secret export and password vault reads.
Deny debugger attach, process memory read, and debug listener creation.
Deny cross-profile and private-session data outside assigned storage scope.
Deny wrong-origin, wrong-profile, wrong-role, and unauthorized broker calls.

probe_targets.purpose: Own graphics device, assigned surfaces, and validated shared memory without profile, network, credential, or arbitrary filesystem authority.
probe_targets.current_evidence: process-capabilities.json allows graphics device, assigned surfaces, and validated shared memory while forbidding profile storage, network sockets, credential store, and arbitrary filesystem.
probe_targets.missing_evidence: No GPU-role platform process, graphics-device policy, shared-memory generation probe, or device-reset denial artifact exists.

probe_matrix.expected_result: Deny arbitrary filesystem and profile storage access.
Deny network sockets and DNS access.
Deny process creation and shell execution.
Deny registry/config writes beyond reviewed driver query policy.
Deny raw devices except assigned graphics device handles under role policy.
Deny unvalidated, stale-generation, executable, or oversized shared memory.
Deny credential store and keychain access.
Deny debugger attach, process memory read, and debug listener creation.
Deny profile storage, history, bookmarks, and cross-profile files.
Deny wrong-surface, wrong-device-generation, wrong-role, and unauthorized service IPC.

probe_targets.purpose: Run hostile codecs, fonts, images, PDFs, archives, or other native parser tasks with task-scoped input and bounded output only.
probe_targets.current_evidence: process-capabilities.json records media_utility as the current decoder-class role with task-scoped input, bounded output, and optional assigned device handles while forbidding ambient network, arbitrary filesystem, profile storage, browser UI, and credential store.
probe_targets.missing_evidence: No packaged decoder/media utility process, hostile-input fixture, codec-specific policy, output-bound proof, or platform sandbox probe exists.

probe_matrix.expected_result: Deny arbitrary filesystem and profile storage outside task-scoped input handles.
Deny ambient network sockets and DNS access.
Deny process creation and shell execution from hostile parser input.
Deny registry and platform configuration access.
Deny raw devices except optional assigned device handles for reviewed tasks.
Deny unbounded output buffers, executable mappings, and stale shared-memory generations.
Deny credential store, keychain, cookies, and provider tokens.
Deny debugger attach, process memory read, and debug listener creation.
Deny profile storage, history, bookmarks, and private-session data.
Deny unauthorized broker calls, wrong task identity, stale task handles, and internal protocol access.

probe_targets.purpose: Run extension APIs and granted host access without kernel memory, ungranted profiles, ambient native messaging, update signing, or agent confirmation authority.
probe_targets.current_evidence: process-capabilities.json records declared extension APIs, granted hosts, and extension storage while forbidding kernel memory, ungranted profiles, ambient native messaging, update signing, and agent confirmation.
probe_targets.missing_evidence: No extension-host process, extension identity fixture, host-grant denial probe, native-messaging broker probe, or incognito policy artifact exists.

probe_matrix.expected_result: Deny arbitrary filesystem and ungranted native messaging paths.
Deny ambient network sockets outside declared and granted extension policy.
Deny process creation and ambient native messaging.
Deny registry/configuration access except reviewed enterprise-policy read paths.
Deny raw devices without declared permission and user approval.
Deny unassigned shared memory and executable mappings.
Deny password vault, cookies outside grants, and agent confirmation authority.
Deny debugger attach and target inspection without explicit DevTools attachment.
Deny ungranted profiles, private sessions, and cross-profile extension storage.
Deny ungranted extension APIs, wrong extension identity, wrong host grant, and update signing IPC.

probe_targets.purpose: Expose attached target protocol and local frontend resources without ambient remote listener, unattached profile access, or release signing authority.
probe_targets.current_evidence: process-capabilities.json allows attached target protocol and local frontend resources while forbidding unattached profiles, ambient remote listener, release signing, and agent confirmation.
probe_targets.missing_evidence: No DevTools attachment authorization probe, remote-listener denial probe, target epoch check, or visible-indicator artifact exists.

probe_matrix.expected_result: Deny arbitrary filesystem and local frontend path escapes.
Deny ambient remote listener and unauthenticated debug ports.
Deny process creation and release tooling execution.
Deny registry/configuration edits and unreviewed developer-mode persistence.
Deny raw devices and capture APIs unless debugging an explicitly attached target with policy.
Deny direct renderer memory and unassigned shared-memory mappings.
Deny credential store and agent confirmation authority.
Deny unattached target debugging, hidden remote listener creation, and process memory read.
Deny unattached profiles and cross-profile debugging state.
Deny wrong target, wrong epoch, unattached target protocol, and release signing IPC.

probe_targets.purpose: Run model adapters, proposed actions, granted observations, and audit append without raw cookies, direct input injection, or confirmation authority.
probe_targets.current_evidence: process-capabilities.json allows granted observations, model adapter, proposed actions, and audit append while forbidding raw cookies, password vault, direct renderer memory, direct input injection, confirmation approval, ungranted files, and ungranted origins.
probe_targets.missing_evidence: No agent-host sandbox process, provider adapter fixture, grant-token probe, prompt-injection compromised-client harness, or confirmation-denial artifact exists.

probe_matrix.expected_result: Deny ungranted files, downloads, uploads, and local path reads.
Deny ambient sockets outside configured model provider or MCP/tool policy.
Deny process creation, direct input injection, and shell execution from model output.
Deny registry/configuration access and policy edits.
Deny camera, microphone, screen, USB, HID, and clipboard device authority without explicit grants.
Deny direct renderer memory and unassigned shared-memory mappings.
Deny raw cookies, password vault, passkeys, tokens, and confirmation approval.
Deny debugger attach, hidden inspection, and raw process memory access.
Deny ungranted origins, profiles, private-session data, and browsing history.
Deny direct action execution, forged grants, stale document epoch, and confirmation bypass IPC.

probe_targets.purpose: Verify signed metadata, stage artifacts, and install version roots without web profile content, browser history, agent data, or arbitrary execution.
probe_targets.current_evidence: process-capabilities.json allows update endpoint, signed metadata, staging directory, and version install roots while forbidding web profile content, browser history, agent data, and arbitrary execution.
probe_targets.missing_evidence: No updater-role sandbox process, no-production-key update lab probe, staged-install denial artifact, or rollback/version-root policy probe exists.

probe_matrix.expected_result: Deny arbitrary filesystem outside staging directory and version install roots.
Deny ambient sockets outside update endpoint policy and signed metadata flow.
Deny arbitrary execution and child process launch not part of reviewed installer policy.
Deny registry/configuration edits outside reviewed install metadata.
Deny raw devices, capture APIs, and unrelated platform resources.
Deny unassigned shared memory and executable mappings.
Deny web profile credentials, browser cookies, agent provider tokens, and signing keys.
Deny debugger attach, memory read, and release signing authority.
Deny web profile content, browser history, and agent data.
Deny wrong-target update commands, unsigned metadata flow, forged version roots, and browser-command IPC.

required_artifacts: effective seatbelt or App Sandbox profile
negative probe logs for each role and surface
platform_evidence_requirements.missing_evidence: No macOS packaged role, signed artifact, effective sandbox profile, entitlement capture, or negative probe result exists.

required_artifacts: job object limits and inherited-handle allowlist
registry, COM/RPC, device, file, socket, and debug negative probe logs
platform_evidence_requirements.missing_evidence: No Windows packaged role, AppContainer/token manifest, job/mitigation capture, inherited-handle inspection, or negative probe result exists.

required_artifacts: user, mount, PID, network, and IPC namespace policy
Landlock or portal policy where applicable
capabilities, cgroups, rlimits, and broker descriptors
file, socket, process, device, shared-memory, credential, debug, profile, and IPC negative probe logs
platform_evidence_requirements.missing_evidence: No Linux packaged role, namespace/seccomp/Landlock manifest, effective-policy capture, or negative probe result exists.

harness_blockers.required_evidence: Each probe must run inside the same packaged process role, launch path, handle set, and platform policy used by the configuration being evaluated.
harness_blockers.safe_failure: Block sandbox-readiness claims when a role cannot launch under its declared policy.

harness_blockers.required_evidence: Probe artifacts must include effective platform policy, token/entitlement/namespace state, handle descriptors, and unsupported/degraded mode markers.
harness_blockers.safe_failure: Reject or label results as design-only when effective policy cannot be captured.

harness_blockers.required_evidence: Probe output must use a stable schema with role, platform, surface, attempted operation, expected result, actual result, error code, artifact hash, and cleanup status.
harness_blockers.safe_failure: Fail closed on ambiguous, missing, skipped, swallowed, or unsupported probe results.

harness_blockers.required_evidence: Probes must use bounded temporary roots, fake credentials, fake profiles, loopback-only network fixtures where needed, and cleanup checks that cannot damage the developer host.
harness_blockers.safe_failure: Abort probes before host mutation if safe roots, fake stores, or cleanup controls are unavailable.

harness_blockers.required_evidence: Brokered allowed operations must have task-scoped fixtures so expected-deny probes distinguish ambient authority from narrowly authorized behavior.
harness_blockers.safe_failure: Hold allowed-operation claims and record only denial coverage when broker scope cannot be proven.

harness_blockers.required_evidence: Harnesses must forge IDs, origins, epochs, handles, paths, sizes, ordering, retries, and disconnected state against each privileged interface.
harness_blockers.safe_failure: Block SEC-GATE-1 and SEC-GATE-6 claims until compromised-client probes execute.

harness_blockers.required_evidence: macOS, Windows, and Linux evidence must name OS version, primitive set, packaging mode, degraded state, unsupported state, and equivalent probe result coverage.
harness_blockers.safe_failure: Block transferring sandbox evidence across platforms or configurations.

harness_blockers.required_evidence: Security and platform owners must review probe semantics, waiver policy, critical failures, host safety, and residual unsupported behavior before promotion.
harness_blockers.safe_failure: Keep PB-012 partial and block security, sandbox, site-isolation, and hostile-browsing claims.
